name: Regex Advanced
description: Advanced Regex Node - Perform powerful regular expression-based text processing including parsing, tokenization, replacement, and pattern matching. Ideal for cleaning text, extracting structured data, validating formats, and feature engineering from string columns.
input: Accepts a DataFrame as input from the previous node.
output: Returns a DataFrame with extracted patterns, tokenized values, match indicators, or transformed text based on the selected regex operation mode.

fields.name: inputCol
fields.title: Input Column
fields.description: Select the text column on which the regular expression operations will be applied.

fields.name: regexPattern
fields.title: Regular Expression Pattern
fields.description: Define the regular expression pattern used for parsing, matching, tokenizing, or replacing text. Standard regex syntax is supported.

fields.name: regexMode
fields.title: Regex Mode
fields.description:
Choose how the regex pattern is applied:
• PARSE - extract groups into new columns
• TOKENIZE_COL - split text into multiple columns
• TOKENIZE_ROW - split text into multiple rows
• REPLACE - replace matched patterns with new text
• MATCH - evaluate whether the pattern matches

fields.name: parseColNames
fields.title: Parse Columns
fields.description: Specify names for the output columns created from regex capture groups. The number of columns should match the number of groups in the pattern.

fields.name: caseSensitive
fields.title: Ignore Case Sensitive
fields.description: Controls case sensitivity of regex matching. Set to false to perform case-insensitive pattern matching.

fields.name: replacementText
fields.title: Replacement Text
fields.description: Text that replaces matched patterns when using REPLACE mode. Supports static text or regex group references.

fields.name: copyUnmatchedText
fields.title: Copy Unmatched Text
fields.description: When enabled, retains original text for rows where the regex pattern does not match. When disabled, unmatched rows may return null or empty values.

fields.name: tokenSplit
fields.title: Split to Columns
fields.description: Specify the maximum number of columns to generate when tokenizing text into columns.

fields.name: tokenizeColName
fields.title: Output Root Name
fields.description: Base name for generated token columns (e.g., token_1, token_2, token_3).

fields.name: extraColumnsPolicy
fields.title: Extra Columns Policy
fields.description:
Defines how extra tokens beyond the specified column count are handled:
• DROP - discard extra tokens with a warning
• DROP_NO_WARN - discard silently
• ERROR - fail execution if extra tokens are found

fields.name: inputMatchCol
fields.title: Match Column Name
fields.description: Name of the output column that indicates whether each row matched the regex pattern (true/false).

fields.name: errorIfNotMatched
fields.title: Error If Not Matched
fields.description: When enabled, the node throws an error if the regex pattern does not match any row. Useful for strict validation workflows.

fields.name: outputColNames
fields.title: Column Names of the Table
fields.description: Explicitly define the names of the output columns generated by the regex operation.

fields.name: outputColTypes
fields.title: Column Types of the Table
fields.description: Specify the data types for the output columns. If left empty, data types are inferred automatically.

fields.name: outputColFormats
fields.title: Column Formats
fields.description: Optional formatting rules for output columns, such as date or timestamp display formats.
